text: The sculptor Constantin Brancusi kept unfinished works covered for months, returning to them only after his mental image had faded. He was exploiting what psychologists call perceptual adaptation — the tendency of sustained exposure to a stimulus to deaden the perceiver's response to it. Familiarity breeds not just contempt but blindness. A writer who reads their draft immediately after writing it sees what they intended, not what is on the page. The gap between a work's composition and its revision is not wasted time; it is the mechanism by which the creator becomes, temporarily, the first reader.
question: What psychological phenomenon does the passage say Brancusi was exploiting by keeping works covered for months?
